name: scoring-discrimination-fix
description: Fix keyword-density scoring noise floor (AUC 0.502 → ≥0.65). Three paths: A) filter high-frequency terms (>30% prevalence), B) fix misclassified terms (哈哈哈≠attack, support≠risk), C) IDF-weighted scoring. Runs in order A→B→C with re-evaluation after each.

steps.title: Path A: Build term frequency table from eval corpus
steps.change: Create server/scripts/buildTermFrequencyTable.js: scan .claude/random_sampling_eval/user_data/ for all 100 users, count per-term user prevalence, output server/data/termFrequency.json with {term, userCount, totalCount, userFraction} per term.
steps.verify: termFrequency.json exists with 300+ terms, '没有' has userFraction ≈ 0.72

steps.title: Path A: Add frequency filter to scoring pipeline
steps.change: In src/languageUnderstanding.js: add MAX_USER_FRACTION=0.30 constant. Before computing axis scores, filter vocabularyMarks to exclude terms where termFrequency[term].userFraction > MAX_USER_FRACTION. Configurable via BILIBILI_TERM_FREQ_THRESHOLD env var.
steps.verify: Re-score user 100015 — '没有'(72% prevalence) should be absent from filtered marks. toxicEmotions drops from 60 → expected 35-45.

steps.title: Path A: Re-evaluate and compare
steps.change: Re-run scoring on all 100 eval users with frequency filter. Run evaluation metrics. Write comparison table to .claude/random_sampling_eval/metrics_pathA.json. Confirm AUC moved up and noise floor dropped.

steps.title: Path B: Fix misclassified terms in dictionary
steps.change: In server/data/deepseekKeywordDictionary.production.json entries: (a) remove or recategorize 哈哈哈 from attack/risk to neutral, (b) remove axis mapping from 笑哭/打call/吃瓜/我觉得 (keep support polarity, drop axis), (c) recategorize 为什么 from evidence/support→逻辑混乱 to neutral. Update .entries/ and .evidence/ subdirectories if they hold per-term files.
steps.verify: Dictionary loads without errors. 哈哈哈 not in attack family. 打call/笑哭 have no axis mapping.

steps.title: Path B: Support-polarity terms must not increase risk scores
steps.change: In src/languageUnderstanding.js axis score computation: only terms with polarity='risk' contribute to axis scores. Support-polarity terms are excluded (or optionally subtract from risk).
steps.verify: User with many support terms (笑哭×32, 打call×20) gets lower or equal risk scores compared to before.

steps.title: Path B: Re-evaluate
steps.change: Re-score all 100 users. Re-run evaluation. Write to .claude/random_sampling_eval/metrics_pathB.json. Compare against metrics_pathA.json and original metrics.json.

steps.title: Path C: Implement IDF-weighted axis scoring
steps.change: In src/languageUnderstanding.js: add computeIdfWeightedScores(vocabularyMarks, termFrequency, N=100). For each risk-polarity mark: weightedCount = count × log(N / userCount). Accumulate per axis. Normalize to 0-100 using per-axis max from reference corpus × 1.1 ceiling.
steps.verify: Without any blocklist, '没有'(df=72) gets idf=0.14× weight. '没文化'(df≈5) gets idf=1.3× weight. Unit test confirms weight ratio ≈ 9:1.

steps.title: Path C: Wire IDF scoring into pipeline and calibrate normalization
steps.change: Replace raw-count accumulation in scoring pipeline with computeIdfWeightedScores(). Compute per-axis max weighted scores from 100-user reference. Set normalization ceiling = max×1.1. Cap at 100.
steps.verify: All 100 users produce scores in 0-100 range. Score distribution has wider spread (std dev increases).

steps.title: Path C: Final re-evaluation and report
steps.change: Full evaluation of IDF-weighted model on 100 users. Write metrics.json (overwrite or to metrics_pathC.json). Generate before/after comparison with all 6 metrics. Update report if targets met.
steps.verify: AUC ≥ 0.65 OR significant improvement from baseline. F1 maintained or improved. Brier/ECE maintained (already good). trollIndex spread ≥ 30. ρ ≥ 0.25.
